candidates.why: Separate operating D&A summary line appears below gross profit and alongside overhead lines, not within or beneath the selected COGS row. This suggests it is not a separate COGS-specific D&A row to add to the selected COGS bucket.
Component of the operating D&A section, but not labelled as COGS/cost of revenues and appears subordinate to the D&A summary rather than a separate COGS-specific add-on.
Explicitly tied to R&D, not COGS, so it should not be added to the selected COGS bucket.

variables.notes: No explicit COGS-specific depreciation/amortisation row is shown above EBIT. The separate D&A lines appear in the operating overhead section after gross profit, so conservatively treat D&A as already embedded in the selected COGS row or otherwise not separately attributable to COGS.

candidates.why: This is the clear operating D&A summary line presented separately from the selected XSGA component rows 'Other External Expenses' and 'Staff Costs'. It sits at the same level as those rows and should be added separately rather than assumed embedded. It also summarizes the more granular D&A lines below, so it is preferred over components to avoid double counting.
Granular component of the D&A section and explicitly operating. Relevant to SG&A/R&D overhead, but a broader D&A summary row exists, so this component should not be selected separately.
Another component within the D&A breakdown. Not selected because the summary D&A row should be preferred over individual components.

variables.notes: OK. Selected the separate operating D&A summary row because XSGA was previously mapped only to 'Other External Expenses' and 'Staff Costs', and this D&A line appears outside those selected rows at the same hierarchy level. No XRD row was selected, so using the summary D&A row is the cleanest way to capture operating D&A without double counting component lines.
